preferred_translation: what one recognizes, one thinks about
alternative_translations: what one recognizes, one directs thinking toward
discouraged_translations: what one perceives, one reasons about
definition: MN 18 sequence line linking recognition to thinking.
notes: MN 18 directly supports this formula. It keeps sañjānāti as recognition-language and vitakka as thinking-language. The line matters because it shows recognition becoming taken-up thought without yet collapsing into proliferation.

example_phrases.translation: what one recognizes, one thinks about
example_phrases.notes: Middle movement of the MN 18 sequence.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the line connecting recognition to thinking in the MN 18 sequence.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'what one recognizes, one thinks about' as the default rendering when this formula is translated directly.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use the controlled alternate only when the English sentence genuinely needs the directional force made more explicit. Do not collapse recognition into generic knowing or vitakka into abstract reasoning-language.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the formula from erasing the distinction between recognition and thinking.
